name: Dynamic and Static Inventory Setup
description: Configure static INI or YAML inventory files and dynamic inventory scripts for cloud providers to manage host groups.

steps.title: Create a static INI inventory
steps.command:
cat > inventory.ini << 'EOF'
[webservers]
web01 ansible_host=192.168.1.10
web02 ansible_host=192.168.1.11

[dbservers]
db01 ansible_host=192.168.1.20

[all:vars]
ansible_user=deploy
ansible_python_interpreter=/usr/bin/python3
EOF
steps.description: Defines host groups with connection details and shared variables.

steps.title: Test connectivity to all hosts
steps.description: Sends a ping module to every host in the inventory to verify SSH connectivity.

steps.title: List hosts in a specific group
steps.description: Shows which hosts belong to the webservers group.

steps.title: Set up dynamic inventory for AWS
steps.description: Uses the aws_ec2 inventory plugin to auto-discover EC2 instances grouped by tags, regions, or instance types.
steps.tip: Create an aws_ec2.yml with 'plugin: amazon.aws.aws_ec2' and configure keyed_groups to organize instances by tags.

steps.title: View the complete inventory graph
steps.description: Displays the full inventory tree with all groups, hosts, and their variables.

fullScript:
# Inventory management
cat > inventory.ini << 'EOF'
[webservers]
web01 ansible_host=192.168.1.10
web02 ansible_host=192.168.1.11

[dbservers]
db01 ansible_host=192.168.1.20

[all:vars]
ansible_user=deploy
ansible_python_interpreter=/usr/bin/python3
EOF
ansible all -i inventory.ini -m ping
ansible-inventory -i inventory.ini --graph --vars

faq.question: Should I use INI or YAML format for inventory?
faq.answer: INI is simpler for small setups. YAML is better for complex inventories with nested groups and structured variables. Both are fully supported.

faq.question: How do I set a default inventory so I don't need -i every time?
faq.answer: Set 'inventory = ./inventory.ini' in your ansible.cfg file under [defaults], or set the ANSIBLE_INVENTORY environment variable.
